evals.prompt: Specify a real-time notification system with WebSocket support
evals.expected_output: Full specification workflow: requirements, solution, classifier-driven decomposition
expectations: Invoked specify-meta to scaffold the spec directory
Created .start/specs/NNN-*/ directory structure
Ran research phase with parallel agents
Invoked specify-requirements skill for requirements.md
Waited for user approval before proceeding to solution
Invoked specify-solution skill for solution.md
Waited for user approval before proceeding to decomposition
Read reference/classifier.md and surfaced rationale before tier selection
Presented Direct, Incremental, and Factory options via AskUserQuestion at step 6
Invoked specify-incremental or specify-factory based on user choice (or skipped if Direct)
Logged the decomposition tier choice in spec README decisions table

evals.prompt: Specify a feature, but skip requirements — start directly from solution design
evals.expected_output: Skip requirements phase, start from solution, continue to decomposition
expectations: Offered user the choice to start from Requirements, Solution, or Decomposition
Accepted user's choice to skip requirements
Logged the skip decision in spec README.md
Proceeded directly to specify-solution
Still continued to decomposition step after solution

evals.prompt: Resume spec 002 which has requirements.md and solution.md completed but no decomposition artifacts
evals.expected_output: Detect existing state and route to classifier for tier selection
expectations: Invoked specify-meta to read spec status
Detected requirements.md and solution.md as completed
Detected no plan/, manifest.md, units/, or scenarios/
Suggested continuing to decomposition step
Did NOT re-run requirements or solution phases

evals.prompt: Run the specify orchestrator on spec 005 (a single-line rate-limit middleware fix). The fixture has requirements.md and solution.md already complete. Reach step 6 and surface the classifier rationale.
evals.expected_output: Classifier recommends Direct tier; rationale cites change_type=fix and minimal surface area
expectations: Read requirements.md before classifying
Read solution.md before classifying
Computed feature_count, ac_count, component_count, change_type
Surfaced the classifier rationale to the user
Recommended Direct tier
Presented all three options (Direct, Incremental, Factory) via AskUserQuestion
Did not auto-invoke specify-incremental or specify-factory without user confirmation

evals.prompt: Run the specify orchestrator on spec 003 (a single-feature notification API with 2 components). Reach step 6.
evals.expected_output: Classifier recommends Incremental tier; rationale cites single feature and 2 components
expectations: Read requirements.md and solution.md before classifying
feature_count == 1 detected
component_count <= 2 detected
Recommended Incremental tier
Surfaced rationale with the specific signals
Presented all three options via AskUserQuestion

evals.prompt: Run the specify orchestrator on spec 006 (multi-feature auth with 4 components, 8 ACs across 3 features). Reach step 6.
evals.expected_output: Classifier recommends Factory tier; rationale cites multi-feature and 4 components
expectations: Read requirements.md and solution.md before classifying
feature_count >= 2 detected
component_count >= 3 detected
Recommended Factory tier
Surfaced rationale with the specific signals
Presented all three options via AskUserQuestion
